verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 encodes the type 3 IP3 receptor, a tetrameric intracellular calcium-release channel localized to the endoplasmic reticulum (and ER-mitochondria contact sites). Its canonical topology has the channel domain embedded in the ER membrane, with both N- and C-termini facing the cytoplasm; the 'extracellular' loops are in the ER lumen, never the plasma membrane outer leaflet. Pre-no checklist: (1) no cell-surface-directed therapeutic program targets ITPR3 protein body; (2) not a shedding target — no ectodomain released to PM outer face; (3) no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to PM outer leaflet documented; (4) no minority PM pool reported — protein resides in ER/ER-mitochondria junctions; (5) no cancer/cell-death outer-leaflet display, no germline-restricted surface display, no activation-induced PM translocation documented for ITPR3 specifically; no lysosomal exocytosis relevance; no stable-surface-attachment story. HGNC group (IP3R family) is canonically endomembrane. Confidence high.
